change_guardrails: treat system permission changes as sensitive
treat background or auto-start behavior changes as sensitive
do not add outbound data transmission or telemetry without explicit product need
do not introduce real secrets, hardcoded keys, or debug backdoors
project_constraints: keep the utility lightweight and low-overhead while monitoring system state
keep monitoring local-only unless outbound transmission is explicitly needed
avoid requiring background or auto-start behavior unless the product clearly needs it

change_history.reason: confirmed default collaboration language as Korean

change_history.change: confirmed project type and product direction
change_history.reason: normalized the project as the nearest interactive-app category and recorded the native macOS utility context
set the local default completion expectation to require passing test and lint checks
allowed broad refactors and aggressive restructuring when they help the project
user was unsure, so the harness set minimal defaults for permissions, background behavior, data transmission, and secret handling
set the default repository verification rule to always require test and lint
normalized the user's preference for autonomous progress into the safe-edits-can-proceed default
captured the user's preference to build the macOS utility in Swift
filled the remaining harness defaults with safe macOS Swift utility conventions based on the interview answers

notes: Inherited browser policy from parent AGENTS.md: prefer OpenChrome first for browser work and E2E; use Playwright directly when an existing Playwright runner is the natural path.
Product direction: gtop is a small macOS GUI utility that monitors CPU, memory, and network status in a compact window.
project_type is normalized to 'webapp' as the nearest stable interactive-app category in this harness schema; the actual target is a native macOS GUI utility.
Definition of done currently requires test and lint to pass. Add typecheck later if the chosen stack/tooling implies a typed workflow.
Change posture is aggressive: substantial refactors are acceptable when they materially improve the utility.
Safe-default sensitive areas for this macOS utility: system permissions, background or auto-start behavior, outbound transmission/telemetry, and any secret handling.
Verification policy is required: test and lint are mandatory for completion.
Approval policy is autonomy-friendly for this new project: safe edits can proceed directly, while explicitly sensitive changes remain subject to the configured guardrails.
Initial stack direction: build the native macOS utility in Swift.
Default commands assume an Xcode project and scheme named `gtop`; update the command strings once the actual project structure exists.
No separate typecheck command is set yet; Swift compile/build already provides type checking, and an extra typecheck command can be added later if the toolchain warrants it.
